methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $12M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 12 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Foundation Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), post-money $3B+ (reported, raise in progress)

data.narrative:
Lumaril is a Seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $12M to date. The broader sector comprises 9 private peers and 1 public comparable, including Foundation Robotics, 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, and Locus Robotics as key reference points.

A peer-median approach proved inapplicable due to insufficient depth in the narrow peer set: while 12 same-sector peers exist, fewer than three met simultaneous criteria for stage alignment, adjacency, and valuation tier comparability. Consequently, analysis relies on stage-progression methodology. Applying a typical 2.5x step-up multiplier from Seed to the next funding stage against the $12M raised baseline yields an implied valuation of $30M.

The triangulated valuation range is $26M to $34M, with a median of $30M. This analysis carries material uncertainty given reliance on a single applicable method and the absence of sufficiently comparable Seed-stage peers in robotics manufacturing. Key variables affecting realized valuation include product differentiation relative to the identified peer set, customer traction metrics not captured here, and market conditions at the subsequent funding event. Investors should treat this range as preliminary pending deeper diligence into unit economics and go-to-market execution.
